Abstract.ClassName.summary: Retrieves the name of the class an object belongs to.
Abstract.ClassName.returns: The method returns a character string representing the name of a class.

Abstract.ClassObject.summary: Retrieves the class object (System.Type) of a class.
Abstract.ClassObject.returns: The method returns the class object of a class.
Abstract.ClassObject.remarks: The X# XPP implementation returns a System.Type object as class object.

Abstract.GetNoIVar.summary: Handles access operations to undefined instance variables.

Abstract.IsDerivedFrom.summary: Checks if an object belongs to or is derived from a particular class.
Abstract.IsDerivedFrom.param: A character string containing the name of the class an object belongs to or is derived from. Alternatively, the class object (System.Type) can be passed instead of the class name.
Abstract.IsDerivedFrom.returns: The method returns .T. (true) if the object executing the method belongs to or is derived from the specified class.
Abstract.IsDerivedFrom.remarks: This method is used to check if an unknown object has features of a known class. This is especially useful for event driven programming or when classes are inherited from other classes.

Abstract.NoMethod.param: The parameters to send to the method.
Abstract.NoMethod.returns: The return value will be interpreted as the return value of the called undefined method.
Abstract.NoMethod.remarks: If an undefined method is called, a runtime error is raised.

Abstract.Notify.param: This parameter receives a numeric value that corresponds with a constant listed in APPEVENT.CH.
The second parameter identifies the situation for which an object is notified. The file DMLB.CH lists define constants that can be used to test in a program which situation occurred.
Abstract.Notify.remarks: There is no need in X# to include the header files. The defines are included as part of the X# runtime.

Abstract.SetNoIVar.summary: Handles assign operations to undefined instance variables.
Abstract.SetNoIVar.returns: The return value of the method is ignored.

ClassDestroy.summary: Remove the class object of class.
ClassDestroy.param: The name of the class whose class object should be deleted, or the class object.
ClassDestroy.returns: The return value is .T. (true) when the class object is removed from memory, otherwise it is .F. (false).
ClassDestroy.remarks:
The function ClassDestroy() removes the class object of a dynamically created class from main memory. Dynamic classes are created during runtime by the ClassCreate() function. They are unknown at compile time. Therefore, they do not have a class function and are represented at runtime of a program only by a class object.
When a program uses a dynamic class, the corresponding class object should be removed from main memory when the class is no longer needed. Otherwise, the class object remains in memory and can be retrieved by the ClassObject() function at any time.
NoteIn X# classes are never really freed from memory. The .Net framework does not allow that. ClassDestroy() does remove the class from the list of active classes. If you recreate the same class later with the same structure then the class definition from the previous defintion is reused.

ClassHelpers.FindClass.summary: Find the ClassObject for a class

doc.ClassHelpers.FullNameTypeFilter: Delegate to filter types in the DynamicAssembly

ClassObject.summary: Retrieves the class object of class.
ClassObject.param: The name of the class whose class object should be returned.
ClassObject.returns: The function returns the class object of the class with the name <cClassName>. The return value is NULL_OBJECT when the class does not exist.

ClassObject.summary: This class returns the ClasssObject for classes, both the classes created at compile time as well as the classes created at runtime. This object allows to access static members and methods late bound Such as Example():Fieldname

ClassObject.New.summary: Create a new instance of the class.

ClassObject.NoIvarGet.summary: Late bound access to class/static vars
ClassObject.NoIvarGet.param: Name of the property/field to read
ClassObject.NoIvarGet.returns: the result of reading the property or field

ClassObject.NoIvarPut.summary: Late bound assign for class/static vars
ClassObject.NoIvarPut.param: Name of the property/field to update

ClassObject.NoMethod.summary: Late bound calls for Static/Class methods.

DataObject.Copy.summary: Creates a dependent shallow copy of this instance
DataObject.Copy.returns: This method returns the new DataObject instance.

DataObject.DefineMethod.param: The name of the method to be defined.
A Function name or codeblock that must be executed when the method is called.
DataObject.DefineMethod.remarks: Dynamic methods in X# are called from the NoMethod method inside the DataObject class.

DataObject.IsMemberVar.summary: Checks if the object has a particular member variable
DataObject.IsMemberVar.param: is a character string containing the symbolic identifier of a member variable.
DataObject.IsMemberVar.returns: The return value is .T. (true) if the object has the member variable <cVarName>, otherwise it returns .F. (false).

DataObject.Merge.summary: Merges exported member variables from another object into this instance.
DataObject.Merge.param: DataObject to copy the member variables from.
Optional prefix that will be added to all fields in the DataObject.

DbCargo.summary: Attaches an arbitrary value to a used work area
DbCargo.param: the value to attach to a work area.
DbCargo.returns: The function returns the value attached to a work area before the function is called.
DbCargo.remarks: The function DbCargo() attaches an arbitrary value to a used work area. The value remains in the work area, In this way, user defined data can be bound to a particular work area. Attached data is discarded when the workarea is closed.

DbDescend.summary: Checks if the order for navigation is descending
DbDescend.returns: The return value of DbDescend() is TRUE when the in the current work area is descending, otherwise FALSE is returned.
DbDescend.remarks: The function DbDescend() tests if the order in the current work area is descending. Refer to DbSetDescend() for more information about reversing the navigational order.

DbSetDescend.summary: Reverses the navigational order of a work area.
DbSetDescend.param: A logical value. When TRUE the navigational order in the current work area is set to descending, FALSE sets the order to ascending.
DbSetDescend.returns: The return value of DbSetDescend() is the previous setting for DbSetDescend().
DbSetDescend.remarks: The function DbSetDescend() is used to quickly change the order in a work area from ascending to descending and vice versa without the need to create a corresponding index. When TRUE is passed to the function, the order is reversed, i.e. all functions and commands that move the record pointer are inverted. DbGoTop() becomes DbGoBottom(), DbSkip(1) becomes DbSkip(-1), EOF becomes BOF() etc. Not all RDDs support switching the order at runtime

DynamicClassObject.summary: The worker class to help create classes at runtime.

doc.LOCKING_EXCLUSIVE: Specifies the locking behaviour inside/outside of transactions

PosAlpha.summary: Retrieves the 1 based position of the first letter in a string.
PosAlpha.returns: Returns the position of the first letter in a string as a numeric value, or ZERO when there is no uppercase letter in the string.
PosAlpha.param: When TRUE is passed then the first character is returned which is not a not a letter. Defaults to FALSE.
Number of characters that must be ignored. Defaults to 0.
PosAlpha.remarks: In X# A letter is defined as a character for which the Char.IsLetter() method returns TRUE. If is set to TRUE the function returns the position of the first character which is not a letter.

PosChar.summary: Replaces a single character at a specified position in a string.
PosChar.param: The new character to insert into the string
1 based position to change. Defaults to Len().

PosDel.summary: Removes characters at a specified position in a string.
PosDel.param: 1 based position to start deleting from. Defaults to Len().
The number of characters to delete.

PosIns.summary: Inserts a character string into a string at a specified position.
PosIns.param: 1 based position where the string must be inserted. Defaults to Len()+1.

PosLower.summary: Retrieves the 1 based position of the first lower case letter in a string.
PosLower.returns: Returns the position of the first lower case letter in a string as a numeric value, or ZERO when there is no uppercase letter in the string.
PosLower.param: When TRUE is passed then the first character is returned which is not changed by function Upper(). Defaults to FALSE.
Number of characters that must be ignored. Defaults to 0.
PosLower.remarks: In X# A letter is defined as a character for which the Char.IsLetter() method returns TRUE. If is set to TRUE the function returns the position of the first character which is not a letter.

PosRepl.summary: Inserts a character string into a string at a specified position.
PosRepl.param: 1 based position where the string must be inserted.
PosRepl.remarks: If the parameter is not specified the function replaces Len( ) characters at the end of .

PosUpper.summary: Retrieves the 1 based position of the first upper case letter in a string.
PosUpper.returns: Returns the position of the first upper case letter in a string as a numeric value, or ZERO when there is no uppercase letter in the string.
PosUpper.param: When TRUE is passed then the first character is returned which is not changed by function Lower(). Defaults to FALSE.
Number of characters that must be ignored. Defaults to 0.
PosUpper.remarks: In X# A letter is defined as a character for which the Char.IsLetter() method returns TRUE. If is set to TRUE the function returns the position of the first character which is not a letter.

SetCollation.summary: Query current and/or set new active collation.
SetCollation.param: the numeric identifier for the collation table to be selected. Must be one of the values from the XppCollations enum of a simple number
SetCollation.returns: The numeric identifier of the active collation table

SetCollationTable.summary: Query current and/or set user defined collation table .
SetCollationTable.param: the numeric identifier for the collation table to be selected. Must be one of the values from the XppCollations enum of a simple number

a one dimensional array with 256 elements. It must contain numeric values representing the weighing factors for single characters. The ASCII value of a character plus 1 defines the array element that contains the weighing factor for that character.
SetCollationTable.returns: a one dimensional array holding the weighing factors of characters for the active collation.

StaticClassObject.summary: This class returns the ClasssObject for classes created at compile time (Static classes) This object allows to access static members and methods late bound Such as Example():Fieldname

XAction.summary: Helper class that stores actions (codeblocks) for XML nodes

XAction.Block.summary: Codeblock to run for the action

XAction.Name.summary: XML node name for which the action runs.

XDocument.summary: Helper class that is used by the XML functions

XDocument.AsXmlArray.summary: Return the children of a node as a XPP specific XML array

XDocument.ClearActions.summary: Clear all actions on a document

XDocument.FindChildTag.summary: Find a child tag in an XML Node

XDocument.FindFirstChildTag.summary: Find a child tag in an XML Node

XDocument.FindNode.summary: Get XML node and document by ID

XDocument.FindParent.summary: Get ID for the parent of an XML node

XDocument.FindTag.summary: Get a list of the tags in an XML Node

XDocument.GetDocument.summary: Find an open XDocument object by its unique handle

XDocument.GetErrorsForDocument.summary: Get error for a specific document

XDocument.OpenText.summary: Open a XML document from a string

XDocument.PerformActions.summary: Perform actions on an XML node.

XDocument.ProcessNodes.summary: Loop through all the actions and process each node that matches the actions

XDocument.SetAction.summary: Set an action on a XML node

XDocument.WalkNode.summary: Walk all nodes and add them to the collections on the document class

XError.summary: Helper class that stores errors for an XML document

XError.DocHandle.summary: Document Handle for which the error occurred.

XError.Id.summary: Id for the node where the error occurred.

XError.ToArray.summary: Convert error object to XBase++ compatible error array.

XMLDocClearErrors.remarks: Closes all XML errors that are not linked to a XML document.

XMLDocClose.returns: Returns .T. (true) if the document is successfully closed and .F. (false) otherwise.
XMLDocClose.remarks: Closes XML document and releases system resources.

XMLDocCloseAll.remarks: Closes all XML documents, clears all errors and releases system resources.

XMLDocGetErrorList.param: Numeric handle of an XML document. Use this handle if a valid document handle was returned by an doc open function.
Numeric error handle returned XMLOpenDocFile() or XMLOpenDocString(). Use this handle to retrieve errors produced by an open function, where an invalid document handle was returned.
XMLDocGetErrorList.returns: Returns a two-dimensional array holding error information, or NULL_ARRAY if no error occurred.

XMLDocGetRootTag.summary: Get the root tag of an XML document.
XMLDocGetRootTag.param: Numeric handle of an XML document.
XMLDocGetRootTag.returns: The function returns the numeric handle of an XML document.
XMLDocGetRootTag.remarks: This function returns the root tag of an XML document. The root tag is a tag that is created by the parser as the root node of the XML document tree. i.e. a document has always a root tag, even if the XML file (or string) is empty. This function provides for the starting point when traversing a document tree. See function XMLGetTag() for more information about an XML tag.

XMLDocOpenFile.param: Name of an XML file to be parsed.
XMLDocOpenFile.returns: Returns a non-zero numeric handle for the document, or 0 if there are not enough system resources for the parser, or when the file cannot be opened, read or parsed. The handle returned by this function must be used in subsequent calls to other XMLDoc..() functions.
XMLDocOpenFile.remarks: XMLDocOpenFile() opens a new XML document. It allocates system resources for the parser, opens and reads the file and parses it contents. While parsing a document, a tree is build by the parsing engine.

XMLDocOpenFile_2.param: Name of an XML file to be parsed.
Pass a variable by reference which receives an error handle if errors were recorded.
XMLDocOpenFile_2.returns: Returns a non-zero numeric handle for the document, or 0 if there are not enough system resources for the parser, or when the file cannot be opened, read or parsed. The handle returned by this function must be used in subsequent calls to other XMLDoc..() functions.
XMLDocOpenFile_2.remarks: XMLDocOpenFile() opens a new XML document. It allocates system resources for the parser, opens and reads the file and parses it contents. While parsing a document, a tree is build by the parsing engine.

XMLDocOpenString.summary: Create XML document from a string.
XMLDocOpenString.param: Character string representing a valid XML document.
Pass a variable by reference which receives an error handle if errors were recorded.
XMLDocOpenString.returns:
Returns a non-zero handle for the passed string or 0 if there are not enough system resources for the parser, or when the string does not contain valid XML code. The handle returned by this function must be used in subsequent calls to other XMLDoc..() functions.
If the function returns 0, more information can be obtained by calling XMLDocGetErrorList(, ).
XMLDocOpenString.remarks: Invokes the XML parser engine which parsed the passed string and builds the internal tree structure.

XMLDocOpenString_2.summary: Create XML document from a string.
XMLDocOpenString_2.param: Character string representing a valid XML document.
Pass a variable by reference which receives an error handle if errors were recorded.
XMLDocOpenString_2.returns:
Returns a non-zero handle for the passed string or 0 if there are not enough system resources for the parser, or when the string does not contain valid XML code. The handle returned by this function must be used in subsequent calls to other XMLDoc..() functions.
If the function returns 0, more information can be obtained by calling XMLDocGetErrorList(,).
XMLDocOpenString_2.remarks: Invokes the XML parser engine which parsed the passed string and builds the internal tree structure.

XMLDocProcess.summary: Execute action code blocks for all tags.
XMLDocProcess.param: Numeric handle for an XML document.
XMLDocProcess.returns: Returns .T. (true) if all action code blocks have been executed successfully or .F. (false) otherwise.
XMLDocProcess.remarks: This function executes all action code blocks registered for an XML document using XMLDocSetAction().

XMLDocResetAction.summary: Releases the action code blocks defined with XMLDocSetAction().
XMLDocResetAction.param: Numeric handle of an XML document.
XMLDocResetAction.returns: Returns .T. (true) if the document handle is a valid and .F. (false) otherwise.
XMLDocResetAction.remarks: This function is used to release all action code blocks defined for all nodes of an XML document

XMLDocSetAction.summary: Set action code blocks for specific tags.
XMLDocSetAction.param: Numeric handle of the XML document.
A character string defining one or more nodes in the XML document. See description below.
A code block to be associated with the nodes in an XML document specified with .
XMLDocSetAction.returns: The function returns the number of tags, or nodes, matching the string .

XMLGetAttribute.summary: Get attribute value of an XML tag.
XMLGetAttribute.param: Numeric handle of the XML tag.
Character string containing the name of the tag attribute whose value is searched.
XMLGetAttribute.returns: Returns a string representing the value of a tag attribute or NULL_STRING if no attribute with the given name exists.
XMLGetAttribute.remarks: This function is used to obtain values of individual XML tag attributes by attribute name.

XMLGetChild.param: Numeric handle for the XML tag.
The tag name of the child to search.
XMLGetChild.returns: Returns the numeric handle of the first child matching the specified name, or 0 if no child with the given name exists.
XMLGetChild.remarks: This function is used to retrieve the first child tag of an XML tag matching the name specified with the second parameter. The XMLGetChildren() function can be used to obtain ALL child tags having a specific name.

XMLGetChildren.summary: Get array of children of an XML tag.
XMLGetChildren.param: Numeric handle for the XML tag.
The tag name of the child tags to search.
XMLGetChildren.returns: Returns an array of child handles matching the name specified as second parameter, or 0 if no child with the given name exists.
XMLGetChildren.remarks: This function is used to retrieve all child tags having the tag name specified with the second parameter.

XMLGetParent.param: Numeric handle for the XML tag.
XMLGetParent.returns: Returns the numeric handle of the parent tag or 0 if doesn't have a parent tag.
XMLGetParent.remarks: This function is used to retrieve the parent tag of an XML tag. Every tag has only one parent, except the document root tag which has no parent tag.

XMLGetTag.summary: Get members of an XML tag node.
XMLGetTag.param: Numeric handle of an XML tag.

If the function returns successfully, this parameter contains an array with all members of the tag. The parameter must be passed by reference. Single elements of the array can be accessed using constants listed below.
XMLGetTag.returns: Returns .T. (true) if the tag is a valid tag and .F. (false) otherwise.
XMLGetTag.remarks: This function is used to retrieve all members of an XML tag node. The elements are the same as in Xbase++, and an extra 6th element has been added that contains the XmlNode object.

XMLSelectNodes.param: Numeric handle for an XML document.
XMLSelectNodes.returns: Returns .T. (true) nDocHandle is recognized or .F. (false) otherwise.

XppCollations.summary: List of possible values for XBase++ collations
XppCollations.remarks: The System enumeration value is represented by 2 different sort orders, depending on the current Ansi or Charmode setting.
